description: R87 fresh10 round-3 closure attempt. Outcome: A.per_rep_recovery_closure; fresh10 reaches recovery_consecutive_rounds=3 via R73 + R86 + R87, per-rep recovery closure achieved. Original cohort C closure NOT claimed; fresh09 remains broken at R85.

summary.accounting_note: divergence_run_count counts runs (a run is a divergence run iff it carries any of the four phase labels app_pre_post_diverged, app_minimal_diverged, minimal_transport_diverged, bridge_io_diverged); divergence_phase_label_count counts phase-label occurrences across those divergence runs (a single run can carry multiple phase labels). label_counts above is per-occurrence, matching divergence_phase_label_breakdown.

interpretation: R87 round-3 closure attempt set: fresh10 only. fresh01, fresh15, fresh09, fresh04 and all other fresh nodes were not run.
All 3 runs completed with matrix_status=0 and label all_ok; no divergence phase labels appeared.
fresh10 now has R73 + R86 + R87 consecutive all_ok and therefore reaches per-rep recovery closure (recovery_consecutive_rounds=3).
Combined with R86 closures for fresh01 and fresh15, the rotated active set fresh01/fresh15/fresh10 all hold per-rep recovery closure.

Original cohort C closure is NOT claimed: fresh09 broke at R85 with 3/3 same_failure(timeout) and was not re-run; it remains the broken rep, and the original cohort C identity (fresh01+fresh09+fresh15) cannot be closed without a fresh re-evaluation of fresh09.

fresh10_round3_closure_status.original_cohort_c_closure_reason: Original cohort C identity is fresh01+fresh09+fresh15. fresh09 broke at R85 with 3/3 same_failure(timeout), reset its recovery chain to 0, and was not re-run in R87. fresh10 closure is a rotated-replacement closure, not a substitute for fresh09's original-cohort membership.

fresh09_status.note: fresh09 was not run in R87 and is NOT recovered. It remains the R85 broken rep.

classification.label: fresh10 per-rep recovery closure achieved (round-3 closure attempt)
classification.rationale: R87 executed only the authorized round-3 closure attempt for fresh10, 3 runs at example.com:80. All 3 matrix runs completed with matrix_status=0 and label all_ok, no phase labels, no NEW structural divergence. fresh10 now has three consecutive all_ok recovery rounds (R73, R86, R87), so per-rep recovery closure is achieved. Combined with fresh01/fresh15 R86 closures, the rotated active set fresh01/fresh15/fresh10 holds per-rep recovery closure end-to-end.
classification.original_cohort_c_closure_reason: fresh09 (the original cohort C member) broke at R85 with 3/3 same_failure(timeout) and was not re-run; it remains broken. fresh10 is a rotated replacement, not a substitute for fresh09 in the original-cohort identity.
classification.next_handling: Do not auto-extend R87. Do not write whole / original cohort C closure. Do not write fresh09 recovered. Next natural authorized round, if desired, would either: (a) draw from R73 round-1-only recovery pool (fresh08/fresh11/fresh12/fresh13/fresh14) for additional rotated coverage, or (b) re-evaluate fresh09 with a fresh independent run to decide whether the R85 timeout was steady-state or noise. Either path requires a new explicit user authorization.

redaction_notes: Evidence uses only the neutral outbound key fresh10.
Raw tag/server/uuid/public_key/short_id/path/header/server_name/password material remains only in local /tmp inputs and is not committed.
subset_schema_gate.violations is committed only because it is the empty array; structural redaction is preserved.
